title: Patrick Williams
content:
People.ContactEmail pwilliams@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2015
- University of British Columbia
Patrick’s focus on the big picture guides clients through their most complicated disputes.
Patrick Williams is a partner in our Litigation Group in Vancouver. He regularly represents clients in high-stakes contract disputes and product liability cases. Patrick also practices administrative and public law and represents clients in disputes with federal, provincial, and municipal governments. Experienced with trials, appeals, arbitrations, and regulatory proceedings, Patrick combines practical advice and fierce advocacy.
Patrick has acted for leading Canadian and multinational companies including 3M Company, Airbnb, Bell, Canadian National Railway, CPPIB, Endo Pharmaceuticals, Glencore, ING Bank, International Motors, Juul Labs, lululemon, Rothmans, Benson & Hedges, TD, TELUS, Uber, Wood Canada, and others.

#### Patrick’s recent experience includes
- acting for claimants and respondents in international and domestic arbitrations related to real estate developments, mineral royalties, energy contracts, shareholder disputes and other commercial matters;
- defending global manufacturing and consumer goods companies in health care cost recovery claims by provincial governments and product liability class actions;
- defending global online platforms and manufacturers in class actions about the display of prices and provision of services;
- defending companies in litigation related to wildfires, including class actions, civil claims, and regulatory proceedings;
- defending automotive companies in product liability cases, including class actions;
- acting for a multinational engineering firm in the litigation arising from the breach of the Mount Polley Tailings Storage Facility; and
- acting for a class of First Nations across Canada in litigation against the Canadian government related to clean drinking water that resulted in an $8 billion settlement.
Patrick has appeared in the Supreme Court of Canada, all levels of court in British Columbia, the Federal Court of Appeal, the Federal Court, domestic and international commercial arbitrations, and before administrative tribunals.
Patrick also represents individuals and non-profit organizations in his pro bono practice. He is lead counsel for the Council of Canadians with Disabilities in a constitutional challenge to provisions of British Columbia’s Mental Health Act and represented the successful appellant, a person with disabilities, in S.A. v. Metro Vancouver Housing Corp. , 2019 SCC 4, the leading Canadian case on discretionary trusts.
Patrick is a contributing author to Defending Class Actions in Canada , published by LexisNexis, and National Editor of the firm’s Canadian Class Actions Monitor blog .
Patrick is Co-Chair of the Vancouver Student Committee and regularly meets with and mentors current and prospective summer and articling students.
Patrick received his BCom (Hons) and JD from the University of British Columbia. He clerked for Chief Justice Bauman at the Court of Appeal for British Columbia.
